item.title: 2016 in review
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
A re짯view of the events that took place this year, re짯lat짯ed to soft짯ware engi짯neer짯ing and ar짯chi짯tec짯ture.

The conferences, talks, and events

These are some of the events I par짯tic짯i짯pat짯ed in:

  • (2 Apr 2016) PRO짯TO짯SEC 2016 [1]: A tech짯ni짯cal con짯fer짯ence about net짯work짯ing, pro짯to짯col짯s, se짯cu짯ri짯ty, and re짯lat짯ed top짯ic짯s.
  • (Ju짯ly 2016) Eu짯roPy짯thon 2016: I at짯tend짯ed sev짯er짯al talk짯s, train짯ings, work짯shop짯s, and pre짯sent짯ed a talk.
  • (10 Sep 2016) Python C처r짯do짯ba meet짯up [6]: in짯ter짯est짯ing meet짯up where I p짯re짯sent짯ed my talk at Eu짯roPy짯thon and at짯tend짯ed the sprints af짯ter짯ward짯s.
  • (14 Sep 2016) Ar짯q짯Conf Meet짯up 쩔C처짯mo es tra짯ba짯jar dis짯tribui짯do glob짯al짯mente? 짯Ca짯so Open Stack [5]: Host짯ed in the Red Hat's of짯fices in Buenos Aires, this talk was a very in짯ter짯est짯ing one, on which I learned about the col짯lab짯o짯ra짯tive way of work짯ing of the Open Stack team, around the globe.
  • (17 Nov 2016) Python Barcelona meet짯up.
  • (9 Dec 2016) Barcelona Spark meet짯up [2]
  • (22 Dec 2016) Python Barcelona De짯cem짯ber meet짯up [7]: I pre짯sent짯ed my talk about clean code in Python (yes, again :-), and there were two oth짯er in짯ter짯est짯ing talk짯s. Mine was the first one of the evening. The sec짯ond talk, was about zc.build짯out, an in짯ter짯est짯ing tool for man짯ag짯ing pack짯ages and en짯vi짯ron짯ments in Python, which I first heard of at Py짯conAr (Python Ar짯genti짯na group) 2012. The third and last talk, p짯re짯sent짯ed a project named aio짯cache [8]: a cache sys짯tem im짯ple짯ment짯ed in asyn짯cio, that sup짯ports mul짯ti짯ple back짯-end짯s. The talk walked us through the 짯code, its ar짯chi짯tec짯ture, and some use cas짯es with demos.

Code wise

I start짯ed with a small con짯tri짯bu짯tion to the coala project [3], and dur짯ing the Eu짯roPy짯thon sprints I con짯trib짯uted to aio짯http with a pull re짯quest.

There were two new re짯leas짯es of my vim con짯fig짯u짯ra짯tion, ver짯sions v0.3 and v0.4 [4], which is great be짯cause is the sin짯gle tool I use ev짯ery day, and it is nice to have sev짯er짯al im짯prove짯ments on it.

I al짯so en짯hanced my dot files, but that is still in be짯ta.

On general

A mi짯nor re짯lease of Python3.5, but most im짯por짯tant짯ly: Python3.6 was re짯cent짯ly re짯leased!

There were two re짯leas짯es of Fe짯do짯ra, 24 and 25, and there were lots of im짯prove짯ments and changes in the Lin짯ux Ker짯nel.

Re짯gard짯ing in짯for짯ma짯tion se짯cu짯ri짯ty, there were some is짯sues in the Lin짯ux Ker짯nel, al짯so in ap짯pli짯ca짯tions (such as dirty COW, etc.), and a mas짯sive DDoS at짯tack that stroke the In짯ter짯net on Oc짯to짯ber, due to IoT de짯vices be짯ing com짯pro짯mised.

What to look forward to for the new year

  • I still look for짯ward for more con짯tri짯bu짯tions to open source, in par짯tic짯u짯lar with CPython.
  • More func짯tion짯al pro짯gram짯ming (S짯cala/Haskell may짯be), low-lev짯el pro짯gram짯ming in짯 C, and re짯search in the field of in짯fos짯ec.
  • Keep on par짯tic짯i짯pat짯ing on tech짯ni짯cal con짯fer짯ences.

And a lot more.

[1]https://www.protosec.org/en/
[2]https://www.meetup.com/Spark-Barcelona/events/235763327/
[3]https://github.com/coala/coala
[4]https://github.com/rmariano/vim-config/releases
[5]https://www.meetup.com/es/ArquitecturaDeSoftware/events/233824352/
[6]https://www.meetup.com/es-ES/Cordoba-Python-Meetup/events/233741709/
[7]https://www.meetup.com/python-185/events/236196808/
[8]https://github.com/argaen/aiocache
